ComponentType: Non-synchronous Step-Down (Buck) DC-DC Converter with Integrated High-Side MOSFET
Description: The TPS54331 is a monolithic buck converter designed to deliver up to 3 A of continuous output current from an input voltage range of 3.5 V to 28 V. It integrates an 80 mΩ high-side MOSFET and employs current-mode control with internal slope compensation, enabling stable operation with ceramic output capacitors. Key features include a fixed 570 kHz switching frequency, pulse-skipping Eco-mode™ for high efficiency at light loads, adjustable output voltage down to 0.8 V, programmable undervoltage lockout (UVLO), and comprehensive protection functions such as cycle-by-cycle current limit, frequency foldback, overvoltage transient protection, and thermal shutdown. It is offered in 8-pin SOIC and thermally enhanced SOIC PowerPAD packages.

KeyParameters.Package Options: SOIC-8 (4.90 mm × 3.90 mm) and SO PowerPAD-8

Functions.FunctionDescription: Connection for bootstrap capacitor to drive the high-side MOSFET; requires 0.1 μF capacitor to PH pin.
Supply input pin; accepts 3.5 V to 28 V.
Enable control; pull below 1.25 V to disable, float to enable. Also used for programming input undervoltage lockout (UVLO).
Soft-start control; connect capacitor to set soft-start time. Can be used for output voltage tracking.
Inverting input of the error amplifier; connect to output voltage divider for regulation.
Output of the error amplifier; connect external compensation network.
Connection to the internal high-side MOSFET drain and external inductor.
